title: fak — four wins, by example
source: worked-examples reveal — companion to docs/showcase.html §by-example; numbers trace to BENCHMARK-AUTHORITY.md + TURN-TAX-RESULTS.md (2026-06-23)

scenes.headline: fak — the agent kernel
scenes.subcaption: Four wins, one worked example each.

scenes.headline: Watch a poisoned turn vanish
scenes.subcaption: Quarantine evicts the span from the middle of a kept run; the cache is left bit-identical to one that never saw it.
scenes.footer: internal/kvmmu · evicted-vs-never max|Δ|=0, poison-vs-never >0 · proven on a synthetic model, oracle-checked vs HuggingFace

scenes.headline: The turn that never fires
scenes.subcaption: More tool calls, more saved: the kernel resolves a malformed, duplicate, or pure call inside the syscall it arrived on.
scenes.footer: turntax-airline, 14 calls · the win grows with how many tool calls the agent makes

scenes.headline: Pay the shared prefix once
scenes.subcaption: The shared system-prompt-and-tools block is prefilled once for the whole fleet and cloned, not re-read every turn of every agent.
scenes.footer: headline-qwen-50x5 · a read-heavy win · numbers trace to BENCHMARK-AUTHORITY.md

scenes.headline: More hooks, sooner
scenes.subcaption: Putting the gate on the call path is what makes “refuse if anything is wrong” the default instead of an aspiration.
scenes.footer: Apple M3 Pro · the ratio is boundary tax, not the decision

scenes.headline: fak — the agent kernel
scenes.subcaption: Four wins, one example each — see them on the showcase.
